states.label: Trunk cigar-shaped; anterior (S1-3) and posterior (S10-11) slightly narrower than median segments

Trunk nearly rectangular with almost parrallel sides from segment 2 to 9; pachycycli often strong and very well-developed; segment 2 often considerably shorter than segment 1 (>20% shorter) and about half of it is covered by segment 1
Slender, nearly rectangular, with broad sternal plates; pachycycli weak to regularly developed
Trunk plump; pachycycli regularly developed; sternal plates rather narrow relative to broad tergal plates
Trunk broader or thicker in anterior end, due to very broad tergal plates on anterior segments, combined with rather narrow sternal plates throughout trunk
Trunk broader in anterior end, due to very broad sternal plates on segments 3 to 6 (7)
Trunk extremely compressed laterally, throughout all segments; very broad tergal plates, very narrow sternal plates
Triangular, gradually extending into thin, flexible tip
Triangular, gradually extending into thin, rigid tip
No well-definined TE, no extending tips
No well-definined TE, but long flexible tips from pectinate fringe present
TE narrowing abruptly into long acicular tips
TE short, pointed, with large, broadly lobate sternal extensions protruding well beyond TE
